overview: Numa provides AI-powered phone answering, originally built for restaurants and adapted for other local businesses including home services. Their core weakness is that phone answering (not text-back) is their primary modality, and their home services expertise is secondary to their restaurant roots.

weaknesses.weakness: Built for restaurants, adapted for home services as secondary market
weaknesses.talk_track: Numa was built to handle restaurant calls -- orders, reservations, hours. They pivoted to home services, but the DNA is different. When a homeowner texts about a gas leak at midnight, you need AI built for home services emergencies, not adapted from pizza ordering.
weaknesses.evidence: Company history, early product positioning, restaurant-focused case studies still prominent

weaknesses.talk_track: Numa's strength is AI phone answering. But here's the problem: homeowners increasingly prefer text. Missed call text-back is what converts leads in 2024. Numa's text features are secondary to their phone product. NeverMiss was built text-first.
weaknesses.evidence: Product positioning centers on phone/voice AI; text features listed as additional capabilities

weaknesses.talk_track: Numa charges per location and requires a demo to get pricing. If you have two locations, you're paying double. NeverMiss is $297 flat -- covers your business, period.
weaknesses.evidence: No public pricing; user reports of per-location billing

weaknesses.weakness: AI phone conversations feel stilted and robotic
weaknesses.talk_track: AI voice conversations are hard to get right -- there are pauses, misunderstandings, and the uncanny valley effect. Your customer calls about a flooding basement and gets a robot voice asking them to repeat themselves. NeverMiss skips that entirely with instant text-back that reads naturally.
weaknesses.evidence: User reviews mention robotic voice quality; AI phone tech still has latency and comprehension issues

weaknesses.talk_track: Numa handles basic routing and FAQs. But when a customer asks if you service a specific neighborhood, or whether you work on Carrier vs Trane systems, or what your weekend emergency rates are -- Numa falls short. NeverMiss learns all of that.
weaknesses.evidence: Demo limitations show generic response patterns for detailed questions

our_advantages.advantage: Purpose-built for home services from day one
our_advantages.proof_point: Every feature designed around home services lead conversion, not restaurant orders
Under 60 second text response vs AI phone conversation with latency issues

our_advantages.advantage: Text avoids uncanny valley of AI voice
our_advantages.proof_point: Text responses read naturally; no awkward AI voice pauses
Learns specific services, service areas, brands serviced, pricing tiers

talk_tracks.when_lead_mentions_numa: Numa does AI phone answering well. But the shift in home services is toward text-back. 78% of homeowners prefer getting a text within a minute over talking to an AI on the phone. NeverMiss was built for text-back specifically, and at $297 flat, there's no per-location pricing.
talk_tracks.when_lead_is_comparing_prices: Numa's pricing isn't public, and it's per-location. If you're running even 2 trucks out of different areas, you might be paying double. NeverMiss is $297 total, flat, month-to-month.
talk_tracks.when_lead_values_phone_answering: Phone answering is important, and Numa handles it. But consider: the fastest growing channel for lead conversion is text-back after missed calls. When someone calls and you miss it, a text back in 60 seconds converts 3x better than a voicemail callback. NeverMiss focuses on that exact moment.
talk_tracks.when_lead_is_current_numa_customer: How is Numa handling your text follow-ups? That's where most of the lead conversion actually happens. A lot of Numa users add NeverMiss to handle the text-back side since Numa is phone-focused. They complement each other, or you can compare conversion rates.

deploy_when: Lead mentions 'AI phone answering' or 'AI receptionist'
Lead mentions restaurant-focused tools being used for home services
Lead asks about voice AI vs text AI
CRM notes indicate current Numa customer
Lead has multiple locations (per-location pricing sensitivity)

counter_objections.numa_answers_actual_phone_calls: It does, and that has value. But the data shows text-back converts more leads than AI phone answering. Both is ideal, but if you pick one, pick the one homeowners prefer: fast text response.
counter_objections.numa_is_ai_too: Both are AI, but built for different things. Numa's AI is optimized for phone conversations. NeverMiss AI is optimized for text-based lead conversion in home services. Different tools for different jobs.
counter_objections.i_want_phones_answered_not_texts: Makes sense. Numa or Smith.ai can handle phones. But layer NeverMiss on top: every missed call gets an instant text-back. You'll be surprised how many leads you recover that even a good phone service misses.
